content.full_text:
June 9, 1992

Ms. Linda Cassidy  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
Technical Analysis & Assistance  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95812-0807

Re: Independent Expenditure

Dear Linda:

This letter requests written confirmation of advice you (and Carla Wardlow) provided me a couple of weeks ago regarding the distinction between "independent expenditures" and "contributions" under the Political Reform Act.

In your advice letter to Judith Davis (No. A-90-173 [4/20/90]), the FPPC indicated that if an individual solicits and receives materials from a candidate and subsequently disseminates the materials, the individual has made a contribution to the candidate rather than an independent expenditure. The individual does not make a contribution, however, if he or she simply disseminates "information contained in the materials." (Davis Advice Letter at p. 5.)

We asked you how this advice letter applies to the situation in which an individual asks a candidate for a photograph, and then independently uses the photograph in a campaign piece. You indicated that the use of the photograph would not render the otherwise independent expenditure a contribution to the candidate. Your advice of course presumed that the individual does not undertake other activities in connection with the piece which break its independence.

Please contact me immediately if this letter does not correctly summarize your advice.

Sincerely,

James R. Sutton
